codeReview.summary: Reviewed the worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 05ce972 diff. The implementation and verification evidence support the feature goals: testnet services were started, /health-/depth-/market checks were run, buy-simulation completed with verified fill and low slippage, frontend data flow was interactively checked, and services were stopped afterward. No high-confidence correctness or security defects were identified in the reviewed diff for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill does not document the Aptos testnet faucet deprecation pattern and deployer-transfer fallback for funding new accounts, even though this was a central blocker in this feature run.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts skeleton (session c0b92879-cb1b-4f65-b182-c3959e68833d) shows repeated faucet-failure handling and workaround iterations (e.g., lines 331, 339, 360, 737 in extracted skeleton search), and handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges explicitly requests adding this guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: WebSocket healthcheck in services.yaml is effectively non-failing and can mask WS startup failures during mission verification flows.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:34 uses `healthcheck: curl -sf http://localhost:3101/health 2>/dev/null || echo "ws check"`, where the fallback `echo` makes the command succeed even if WS health is unreachable.

summary: Feature verify-live-demo passes review: evidence in handoff/transcript and commit diff supports end-to-end live-demo verification on testnet without high-confidence blocking defects. Added shared-state observations for skill guidance (faucet deprecation) and WebSocket healthcheck robustness.
